orig.description: The autonomous driving stack shall support automated vehicle motion by providing localization, planning, and control capabilities that exchange usable state and command information.

usecase.description: The autonomy stack supports continuous automated vehicle motion.
usecase.pre-condition: The vehicle is in autonomous mode and the required sensors are available.
usecase.main-event: The localization system computes a valid pose estimate.
The localization system publishes the most recent pose estimate for downstream consumers.
The planning system generates a trajectory from the current pose.
The control system computes a control command from the selected trajectory.
The vehicle actuators receive the control command.
usecase.post-condition: The autonomy stack continues automated motion with current pose and command information.
usecase.alternate-event: If localization data is invalid or stale, the autonomy stack cannot continue nominal automated motion.

constraint.description: The localization system shall compute a valid pose. Imported from Doorstop requirement SYS0001. The source rationale states that planning and control require a valid pose.
constraint.rationale: Planning and control depend on a usable vehicle pose estimate. This requirement captures the stakeholder intent that downstream autonomy functions must not operate without localization output that the system considers valid.

performance.description: The localization system shall make the most recent pose estimate available at x Hz.
performance.rationale: Downstream consumers need timely localization data rather than stale state information. This requirement reflects the intent that planning and control receive fresh pose updates at a defined interface rate.

constraint.description: The control system shall compute an accurate control command. Imported from Doorstop requirement SYS0003. The source explicitly marks this requirement as non-verifiable because no accuracy metric or operating condition is defined.
constraint.rationale: The stakeholder intent is that the controller produce commands accurate enough for safe and effective vehicle motion. This captures the expected outcome behind the requirement even though the imported source does not yet define measurable acceptance limits.

behavior.description: Support automated motion through localization, planning, and control.

function.description: The localization function shall compute a valid pose estimate for downstream autonomy functions.
The localization function shall publish the most recent pose estimate to downstream consumers.
The control function shall compute a control command from the selected trajectory.

verification.description: 1. Operate the localization system with representative sensor inputs. 1. Confirm that the system outputs a pose estimate usable by downstream planning and control interfaces. 1. Record the conditions under which the pose is considered valid.

1. Operate the localization system while sampling the published pose interface. 1. Measure the update rate of the most recent pose estimate. 1. Confirm that the measured update rate meets the allocated x Hz requirement.

1. Review the control command requirement and identify the missing accuracy metric and operating conditions. 1. Define measurable acceptance criteria before attempting a formal verification procedure. 1. Reclassify this item to a test or analysis method once the requirement becomes verifiable.

architecture.uml:
node "Autonomy stack" { [Sensors] -- [Localization subsystem] -- [Localization subsystem] -- [Planner] -- [Planner] -- [Controller] -- [Controller] -- [Vehicle actuators] -- }
node "Vehicle operator" as Operator Operator ..> [Planner]: supervise
architecture.description: A logical autonomy stack linking localization, planning, control, and vehicle actuation.

interface.description: The localization subsystem publishes the most recent vehicle pose estimate to the planner.
interface.software: The interface shall provide the most recent pose estimate at the allocated publication rate.

interface.description: The planner provides a selected trajectory to the controller.
interface.software: The interface shall communicate the current trajectory selected for autonomous motion.

interface.description: The controller provides control commands to the vehicle actuators.
interface.software: The interface shall communicate steering, throttle, and braking commands to the actuation layer.

interface.description: Vehicle sensors provide observations to the localization subsystem.
interface.software: The interface shall provide the sensor observations required for localization.
